# Nvidia-supported SiFive reaches a $3.65 billion valuation with its open AI chip technology

[101 finance](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/11/nvidia-backed-sifive-hits-3-65-billion-valuation-for-open-ai-chips/)2026/04/11 14:12

By: 101 finance

## SiFive Secures $400 Million in Funding, Reaching $3.65 Billion Valuation

SiFive, established in 2015 by engineers from UC Berkeley who pioneered an open-source chip architecture, has successfully raised an impressive $400 million in an oversubscribed funding round, bringing the company’s valuation to $3.65 billion.

### Why This Investment Matters

This development is notable for several reasons. Unlike the dominant x86 and ARM processors that power most of today’s AI hardware, SiFive’s RISC-V architecture offers an open alternative. These two established CPU types are the backbone of Nvidia’s AI computing systems, making SiFive’s approach a significant departure from the norm.

### Major Backers and Investors

Nvidia participated in this funding round, joining a diverse group of venture capitalists, private equity firms, and hedge funds. The round was led by Atreides Management, founded by former Fidelity executive Gavin Baker, who also invested in Cerebras Systems’ $1 billion round. Other notable investors include Apollo Global Management, D1 Capital Partners, Point72 Turion, T. Rowe Price, Sutter Hill Ventures, and several others.

### SiFive’s Licensing Model

SiFive operates similarly to how Arm once did, by licensing its chip blueprints to companies who then adapt them for their own products, rather than manufacturing chips themselves. In contrast, Arm shifted its strategy in March by launching its first in-house AI chip, developed in partnership with Meta and adopted by clients such as OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare.

### Open and Independent Chip Design

SiFive distinguishes itself with open, non-proprietary chip designs that remain vendor-neutral and aren’t tied to specific customers. According to Pitchbook, the company’s last fundraising was in March 2022, when it secured $175 million led by Coatue Management at a pre-money valuation of $2.33 billion. That round also included Intel Capital, Qualcomm Ventures, and Aramco Ventures.

Markdown Content:
# Introducing Mistral Small 4 | Mistral AI

Products[Solutions](https://mistral.ai/solutions)[Research](https://mistral.ai/models)[Blog](https://mistral.ai/news)[Customers](https://mistral.ai/customers)Company

[Contact Sales](https://mistral.ai/contact)

[Try Studio](https://console.mistral.ai/?utm_source=website&utm_medium=header_cta)

# Introducing 

 Mistral Small 4

A fast instruct model

A powerful reasoning engineer

A multimodal assistant

Today, we are announcing Mistral Small 4. This model is the next major release in the Mistral Small family. Mistral Small 4 is the first Mistral model to unify the capabilities of our flagship models, Magistral for reasoning, Pixtral for multimodal, and Devstral for agentic coding, into a single, versatile model. With Small 4, users no longer need to choose between a fast instruct model, a powerful reasoning engine, or a multimodal assistant: one model now delivers all three, with configurable reasoning effort and best-in-class efficiency.

Mistral Small 4 is released under the Apache 2.0 license, continuing our commitment to open, accessible, and customizable AI.

## A new standard for multimodal, reasoning-optimized models

Mistral Small 4 is a hybrid model optimized for general chat, coding, agentic tasks, and complex reasoning. Its architecture supports both text and image inputs, making it versatile for a wide range of applications. With Mistral Small 4, we reaffirm our commitment to open-source models and are proud to join the [NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition](https://mistral.ai/news/mistral-ai-and-nvidia-partner-to-accelerate-open-frontier-models) as a founding member, advancing collaboration and innovation in AI development.

### Key architectural details

*   Mixture of Experts (MoE): 128 experts, with 4 active per token, enabling efficient scaling and specialization.

*   119B total parameters, with 6B active parameters per token (8B including embedding and output layers).

*   256k context window, supporting long-form interactions and document analysis.

*   Configurable reasoning effort: Toggle between fast, low-latency responses and deep, reasoning-intensive outputs.

*   Native multimodality: Accepts both text and image inputs, unlocking use cases from document parsing to visual analysis.

### Performance highlights

*   40% reduction in end-to-end completion time (latency-optimized setup).

*   3x more requests per second (throughput-optimized setup) compared to Mistral Small 3.

![Image 3: Performance Comparison Across Internal Models](https://cms.mistral.ai/assets/7d5b181d-776d-4406-aabc-b88414edf567.png?width=2429&height=1787)

## Why Mistral Small 4?

### Unified capabilities

https://openai.com/index/cloudflare-open.title: Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI
https://openai.com/index/cloudflare-open.text:
URL Source: https://openai.com/index/cloudflare-openai-agent-cloud

Markdown Content:
Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI | OpenAI

Listen to article

**Key Takeaways:**

*   Millions of enterprises can now access OpenAI frontier models directly within Cloudflare Agent Cloud.
*   With OpenAI, enterprises using Cloudflare’s Agent Cloud can deploy agents powered by models like GPT‑5.4 to perform real work.
*   Enterprises can now deploy agents built on Codex harness to Cloudflare.

Cloudflare is expanding access to OpenAI frontier models, including [GPT‑5.4⁠](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4/), making them available to millions of customers across Agent Cloud. Agent Cloud is a platform that enables businesses to deploy AI agents powered by OpenAI models to perform real work. For example, companies can use it with OpenAI to deploy agents that automatically handle tasks like responding to customers, updating systems, and generating reports - all within a secure, production-ready environment.

Agent Cloud runs on top of [Cloudflare Workers AI⁠(opens in a new window)](https://www.cloudflare.com/developer-platform/products/workers-ai/), the company’s platform for running AI models at the edge, making it easy for enterprises to build and deploy AI applications and agents that deliver fast, real-time experiences at global scale.

"By bringing OpenAI’s powerful models directly into the Cloudflare environment, we are collapsing the distance between intelligence and the end user. This allows developers to build sophisticated, AI-driven applications and agents that aren't just smart, but are lightning-fast and globally scalable by default. Whether it’s using Codex to streamline development workflows or deploying AI applications and agents at scale, we’re proud to provide the infrastructure that makes the next generation AI-native stack possible," said Dane Knecht, chief technology officer at Cloudflare.

The expansion of OpenAI within Agent Cloud builds on Cloudflare’s broader push to bring the most-advanced AI capabilities, including tools like [Codex⁠](https://openai.com/codex/), to enterprises. The Codex harness is now generally available in Cloudflare Sandboxes, a secure virtual environment where developers can build, run, and test their AI applications. It will also be available in Workers AI in the near future.

“Cloud agents are quickly becoming a foundational building block for how work gets done, and with Cloudflare, we’re making it dramatically easier for developers to deploy, production-ready agents powered by GPT‑5.4 and Codex to run real enterprise workloads at scale,” said Rohan Varma, product, Codex, at OpenAI.

https://markets.financialcontent.com/sto.title: Meta Unleashes Llama 5: Zuckerbergâs Open-Source Gambit Challenges Proprietary AI Dominance
https://markets.financialcontent.com/sto.text:
URL Source: https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/marketminute-2026-4-8-meta-unleashes-llama-5-zuckerbergs-open-source-gambit-challenges-proprietary-ai-dominance

Published Time: 2026-04-08T21:15:54.000Z

Markdown Content:
In a move that has sent shockwaves through Silicon Valley and the global financial markets, Meta ([NASDAQ: META](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/quote?Symbol=NQ%3AMETA)) CEO Mark Zuckerberg officially announced the release of Llama 5 today, April 8, 2026. This latest iteration of the company’s open-source large language model (LLM) represents a watershed moment in the artificial intelligence arms race. Zuckerberg, speaking from the Meta AI Connect summit, claimed that Llama 5 not only matches but exceeds the performance of leading proprietary models, including OpenAI’s GPT-5 and Google’s Gemini 2.0, across critical benchmarks in reasoning, coding, and autonomous agentic behavior.

The immediate implications for the market are profound. By providing a "frontier-class" model with open weights, Meta is effectively attempting to commoditize the very technology that its competitors are trying to sell behind expensive API paywalls. Shares of Meta rose 4.2% in early trading as investors weighed the long-term strategic advantages of Meta's "Linux of AI" strategy against the massive $45 billion capital expenditure the company has committed to its AI infrastructure over the past year.

## The Dawn of Llama 5: A New Benchmark for Intelligence

The release of Llama 5 follows a meticulously planned timeline that began with the disruptive success of Llama 3 in 2024 and the multimodal breakthroughs of Llama 4 in early 2025. Unlike its predecessors, Llama 5 was trained on a state-of-the-art compute cluster powered by over 500,000 NVIDIA ([NASDAQ: NVDA](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/quote?Symbol=NQ%3ANVDA)) Blackwell B200 GPUs. The technical specifications are staggering: the flagship model boasts over 600 billion parameters and introduces "Recursive Self-Improvement" capabilities, allowing the model to refine its own internal logic and generate high-quality synthetic data to fill gaps in its knowledge base.

Key stakeholders in this launch extend beyond Meta itself. The announcement included a collaborative "Llama 5 Launch Day" partnership with cloud giants and hardware manufacturers. Zuckerberg emphasized that Llama 5 was designed with "System 2 thinking"—a cognitive science term referring to slow, deliberate reasoning—enabling the model to solve complex multi-step problems that previously required human oversight. Initial market reactions from the developer community have been overwhelmingly positive, with early testers reporting that Llama 5’s ability to handle long-context windows (up to 5 million tokens) makes it a superior choice for enterprise-level applications compared to more restrictive proprietary alternatives.

[AI](https://www.morningbrew.com/tag/ai)
# OpenAI’s latest release is a social contract revamp

The AI company floated ideas for policies the government can use to mitigate disruptions from its product.

![Image 5: Sam Altman](https://morningbrew.com/cdn-cgi/image/width=1000,height=667,quality=80,format=auto,dpr=1/https://storage.morningbrew.com/image/2026-04-06/image-d8ae7216efd1d73f37f5ea045730c225c6e559e8-1500x1000-jpg/MBD_Altman_SM_04062026.jpg)

Morning Brew Design, Photo: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images

By[Sam Klebanov](https://www.morningbrew.com/contributor/semik97)

April 7, 2026
• less than 3 min read

OpenAI is handing the government a cheatsheet on how to solve the problems its product might cause. Yesterday, the company released a [policy blueprint](https://www.businessinsider.com/openai-superintelligence-ai-upheaval-tax-shorter-workweek-public-wealth-fund-2026-4) to address the disruptions it expects impending AI superintelligence to bring—with tips ranging from embracing a four-day workweek to strategies on combating killer robots.

OpenAI’s [policy menu](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/561e7512-253e-424b-9734-ef4098440601/Industrial%20Policy%20for%20the%20Intelligence%20Age.pdf) comes as the public grows increasingly concerned about AI’s potential to cause economic chaos, with some asking the government to step in. The company is also facing questions about CEO [Sam Altman’s character](https://x.com/RonanFarrow/status/2041213917611856067?s=20), given the potential disruption its product could cause.

## New Deal 2.0

https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.title: Grok 5 Release Date: Latest News & What We Know (April 2026)
https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.text:
URL Source: https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/grok-5-release-date-latest-news-2026

Published Time: 2026-03-24T00:00:00.000Z

Markdown Content:
## The Latest on Grok 5 (March 2026 Update)

Grok 5 has not been released as of March 2026. [Elon Musk originally confirmed](https://phemex.com/news/article/elon-musk-confirms-grok-5-launch-for-q1-2026-36075) a Q1 2026 launch, but that window has passed. xAI's official X account now points to **Q2 2026** as the most likely release window. The model carries a reported [6 trillion parameters](https://news.aibase.com/news/22865) using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and is training on xAI's [1-gigawatt Colossus 2 supercluster](https://www.teslarati.com/elon-musk-xai-brings-1gw-colossus-2-ai-training-cluster-online/) in Memphis, Tennessee. For context, its predecessor [Grok 4 scored 88% on GPQA Diamond](https://x.ai/news/grok-4) and 25.4% on Humanity's Last Exam.

Here is what has happened in the last 30 days:

*   **March 3, 2026** -- xAI shipped Grok 4.20 Beta 2 with five targeted fixes: better instruction following, fewer hallucinations, enhanced LaTeX support, more accurate image search, and improved multi-image rendering. This is the current publicly available model, not Grok 5.
*   **February 25, 2026** -- Grok's official X account updated its projection, pointing to **Q2 2026** as the most likely window for Grok 5's full release.
*   **February 17-18, 2026** -- Grok 4.20 Beta launched with a 4-agent system, followed immediately by Grok 4.20 Heavy with 16 specialized agents. These multi-agent architectures are widely viewed as stepping stones toward Grok 5.
*   **January 2026** -- xAI confirmed Colossus 2, its 1-gigawatt supercluster in Memphis, Tennessee, is fully operational and actively training Grok 5.

The bottom line: Grok 5 missed its Q1 deadline. The new consensus points to Q2 2026.

* * *

## Expected Timeline: When Is Grok 5 Coming Out?

Based on all available signals, here is the most likely timeline:

| Milestone | Expected Window |
| --- | --- |
| Training completion | April 2026 |
| Internal testing / red-teaming | April-May 2026 |
| Public beta (X Premium+) | May-June 2026 |
| Full API access | Q3 2026 |

**Why Q2?** Three key factors point to this window:

1.   **Colossus 2 upgrade to 1.5GW in April**: Musk confirmed that the supercluster is expanding from 1GW to 1.5GW by April 2026. This upgrade aligns with the completion of Grok 5's primary training run -- the additional capacity would support fine-tuning and inference at scale.

2.   **Polymarket odds**: The prediction market gives a 33% probability that Grok 5 ships by June 30, 2026, and only 1% by March 31. The smart money says Q2.

*   Description Cloudflare sets this cookie to track users across sessions to optimize user experience by maintaining session consistency and providing personalized services 

*   Cookie wpEmojiSettingsSupports 
*   Duration session 
*   Description WordPress sets this cookie when a user interacts with emojis on a WordPress site. It helps determine if the user's browser can display emojis properly. 

*   Cookie cookieyes-consent 
*   Duration 1 year 
*   Description CookieYes sets this cookie to remember users' consent preferences so that their preferences are respected on subsequent visits to this site. It does not collect or store any personal information about the site visitors. 

*   Cookie _GRECAPTCHA 
*   Duration 6 months 
*   Description Google Recaptcha service sets this cookie to identify bots to protect the website against malicious spam attacks. 

*   Cookie VISITOR_PRIVACY_METADATA 
*   Duration 6 months 
*   Description YouTube sets this cookie to store the user's cookie consent state for the current domain.  

*   Cookie cookietest 
*   Duration session 
*   Description The cookietest cookie is typically used to determine whether the user's browser accepts cookies, essential for website functionality and user experience. 

*   Cookie rc::a 
*   Duration Never Expires 
*   Description This cookie is set by the Google recaptcha service to identify bots to protect the website against malicious spam attacks.

https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/

Markdown Content:
Meta unveiled Muse Spark on April 8, 2026, its first AI model from the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs. The natively multimodal model handles text, images, audio, and tools, competing with leading systems from OpenAI and Google on benchmarks like GPQA and HealthBench.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Model Capabilities and Reasoning Modes

Muse Spark features three reasoning modes: Instant for quick responses, Thinking for chain-of-thought on harder problems, and Contemplating, which deploys multiple agents in parallel to solve complex tasks faster than sequential methods. It supports visual chain-of-thought, allowing step-by-step reasoning on images, and multi-agent orchestration for tasks like trip planning with subagents handling itineraries and activities simultaneously.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") The model was trained with reinforcement learning that penalizes excessive thinking time, enabling efficient performance on math and science problems.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Benchmark Performance and Health Focus

On HealthBench Hard, Muse Spark achieved 42.8, surpassing GPT-5.4 at 40.1 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 20.6, with training data curated by over 1,000 physicians for health reasoning like nutrition and drug information.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") It excels in visual STEM tasks, generating interactive displays and minigames, and performs strongly in multimodal reasoning under independent evaluation.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Deployment and Infrastructure Overhaul

# Cyber capabilities in Anthropic, OpenAI products put critical infrastructure on a countdown

Apr 10, 2026 - [Technology](https://www.axios.com/technology)

# Frightening AI advances speed race to secure critical infrastructure

![Image 1](https://www.axios.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.axios.com%2FcsbomMKRj0kPnUXAWXrApyCaAI0%3D%2F52x0%2Fsmart%2F2024%2F02%2F14%2F1707954926919.jpg&w=128&q=75)

*   [Sam Sabin](https://www.axios.com/authors/ssabin)

*   email (opens in new window) 
*   sms (opens in new window) 
*   facebook (opens in new window) twitter (opens in new window) linkedin (opens in new window) bluesky (opens in new window) 

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

Add Axios as your preferred source to

see more of our stories on Google.

[Add Axios on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=axios.com)

![Image 2: Illustration of a clock with binary code with the hands nearly at midnight](https://images.axios.com/stofkWNutXIUZvqv0Yvyc8STM7Y=/0x0:1920x1080/1920x1080/2026/02/13/1771014580940.jpeg?w=3840)

Illustration: Sarah Grillo/Axios

Anyone charged with securing America's critical infrastructure — water, electricity, health care, financial services and more — is officially on the clock.

**Why it matters:**AI models are getting so good at autonomously hacking and exploiting severe bugs that their own creators are holding them back out of fear they'll wreak havoc on the systems that power daily life.

*   Anthropic is limiting access to its Mythos Preview model for that reason. OpenAI is looking to do the same for a forthcoming release, a source familiar with those plans told Axios.
*   But such capabilities won't remain locked down for long.
*   Already, researchers have found readily available [open-weights models](https://aisle.com/blog/ai-cybersecurity-after-mythos-the-jagged-frontier)that are capable of exploiting many of the decades-old bugs that Mythos Preview targeted in testing. 

**Driving the news:**Anthropic said Tuesday it has no plans to release its [Mythos Preview model](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-mythos-preview-cybersecurity-risks) to the general public, and other models in the Mythos line won't come up unless strict guardrails are in place and defenders have had more time to prepare.
